entries.title: Run Summarizer Refinement Lessons: Meta Lesson
entries.lesson: Refine a skill by rerunning the same concrete input before and after the edit, then compare output shape and evidence handling.
entries.evidence: The same target run `runs/run-20260320191724-fff0f529.json` produced a looser first summary and a tighter revised summary after only the skill instructions changed.
entries.reuse: When improving a skill, keep the test case fixed, change only the skill, and compare the two outputs before promoting the lesson.

entries.title: Run Summarizer Refinement Lessons: Output Contract Lessons
entries.lesson: Always include a dedicated gaps field that lists unperformed verifications and unconfirmed assumptions.
entries.evidence: The revised summary adds `gaps: No live build or import/CLI verification; existence of packages/moltfarm/cli.py was not confirmed.` while the initial summary had no explicit gaps field.
entries.reuse: Require a `gaps` field in summary-style skills whenever the run may leave important checks unperformed.

entries.title: Run Summarizer Refinement Lessons: Output Contract Lessons
entries.lesson: Include artifact paths and run ids where they materially improve traceability.
entries.evidence: The revised summary names the target run id in `happened` and points to the source run record and log in `produced`.
entries.reuse: When summarizing a prior run, cite the source run id and the main artifact paths if they fit cleanly in the output contract.

entries.title: Run Summarizer Refinement Lessons: Output Contract Lessons
entries.lesson: Keep the field set fixed and ordered so outputs are easy to scan and compare across runs.
entries.evidence: The revised summary uses `attempted`, `happened`, `status`, `produced`, `gaps`, `next_step` and drops the extra header and top metadata block.
entries.reuse: Prefer one stable output contract per skill instead of optional headers or ad hoc metadata sections.

entries.title: Run Summarizer Refinement Lessons: Output Contract Lessons
entries.lesson: Make `next_step` one concrete, low-effort verification action instead of a branching plan.
entries.evidence: The revised summary narrows the follow-up to `pip install -e .` and `molt --help`, while the initial summary mixed layout decisions with installation and verification.
entries.reuse: Prefer one immediate smoke test or validation step that the user can run without additional planning.

entries.title: Run Summarizer Refinement Lessons: Output Contract Lessons
entries.lesson: State explicitly when no build, install, or file mutation happened.
entries.evidence: The revised summary says `no files changed and no distributions built`, which is more informative than only saying no code changed.
entries.reuse: In `produced`, name the absence of expected side effects when that absence changes how the output should be interpreted.

entries.title: Run Summarizer Refinement Lessons: Output Contract Lessons
entries.lesson: Treat recorded run status as transport truth, not a synonym for success.
entries.evidence: The first summary rewrote `completed` as `success (completed)`. The revised summary preserves `status: completed` and leaves outcome detail to the other fields.
entries.reuse: Preserve the run's recorded status verbatim unless the system has a separate evaluated outcome field.

entries.title: Docker Smoke Test Refinement Lessons: Artifact Ownership Lesson
entries.lesson: When a bind-mounted container run should write artifacts back to the host, include `--user "$(id -u):$(id -g)"` by default.
entries.evidence: The successful bind-mounted smoke run used `--user "$(id -u):$(id -g)"` so output files landed in the working tree with the host user's ownership instead of root ownership.
entries.reuse: If a smoke test is expected to create host-visible files under a bind mount, prefer a host-user mapping unless the prompt clearly says ownership does not matter.

entries.title: Docker Smoke Test Refinement Lessons: Narrowness Lesson
entries.lesson: Distinguish stdout-only checks from artifact-writing checks explicitly in the output contract.
entries.evidence: The repo validation had two different container patterns: CLI help and `unittest` runs were stdout-first, while bind-mounted workflow runs were artifact-first and needed a host path to inspect.
entries.reuse: In Docker test recommendations, tell the user whether the post-run inspection target is `stdout` or a host path, and only add a bind mount when the latter is needed.

entries.title: Docker Smoke Test Refinement Lessons: Reuse Lesson
entries.lesson: Reuse a known-good image name when the prompt already provides one.
entries.evidence: `moltfarm-skillbuilder:verify` was enough for container smoke runs after the initial image build, so rebuilding would have added cost without improving the check.
entries.reuse: Prefer `Build: none` when the prompt already names a usable image and the verification target is the run behavior, not the build itself.

entries.title: Codex Skill System Harness Lessons: Canonicalization Lesson
entries.lesson: Normalize external run logs into one canonical event stream before extracting skill evidence.
entries.evidence: The analyzer only became reliable across both flat Codex `--json` logs and archived `~/.codex/archived_sessions` logs after the normalization layer was added behind `analyze_codex_jsonl`.
entries.reuse: When a feature consumes multiple artifact formats, split the work into `load -> normalize -> extract` so the extraction logic stays format-agnostic.

entries.title: Codex Skill System Harness Lessons: Manifest Lesson
entries.lesson: Put real regression cases behind a tracked manifest instead of relying on ad hoc manual reruns.
entries.evidence: `tests/system/codex_skill_corpus.json` captured the concrete log paths and expected skill orders, which turned the external log corpus into a repeatable pass/fail system check.
entries.reuse: For parser and trace-analysis work, track a small manifest of real cases with explicit expectations so regressions fail in a repeatable way.

entries.title: Codex Skill System Harness Lessons: Negative Case Lesson
entries.lesson: Every real-log detection harness needs at least one real negative case where the correct answer is zero invocations.
entries.evidence: The archived-session case passed only because the analyzer ignored skill paths embedded in wrapper text and instructions, instead of mistaking them for actual skill use.
entries.reuse: Pair positive cases with at least one real artifact that contains tempting false signals, and lock the expected result to zero.

entries.title: Codex Skill System Harness Lessons: Observable Evidence Lesson
entries.lesson: Treat explicit skill-file reads as stronger evidence than agent self-report.
entries.evidence: The Game Lab cases passed because the harness counted `.../skills/.../SKILL.md` path reads directly, while agent claim text remained secondary and non-invoking.
entries.reuse: Count file and resource reads as invocation evidence first, then keep free-text claims as supporting evidence rather than the primary signal.

entries.title: Codex Skill System Harness Lessons: Order Preservation Lesson
entries.lesson: Preserve source order exactly when one command references multiple skills.
entries.evidence: The `gamelab-autotrigger` case only became meaningful once the analyzer emitted `develop-web-game` and then `playwright` from a single shell command in left-to-right order.
entries.reuse: When multiple evidence hits come from one command or record, emit them in source order instead of collapsing or re-sorting them.

entries.title: Conversational Eval Authoring Lessons: Additive Merge Lesson
entries.lesson: Generated eval authoring should preserve existing canonical cases verbatim and append normalized new cases instead of rewriting the suite wholesale.
entries.evidence: The draft builder keeps prior `evals/evals.json` cases, de-duplicates generated case ids with suffixes such as `-2`, and avoids overwriting existing fixture files by renaming generated draft fixtures when needed.
entries.reuse: Prefer append-and-normalize behavior for generated eval content unless the user explicitly asks for destructive rewrite semantics.

entries.title: Conversational Eval Authoring Lessons: Draft-First Lesson
entries.lesson: Conversational eval creation should write a reviewable draft workspace before it touches canonical `evals/` files.
entries.evidence: The implemented `create-evals` flow writes `session.json`, probe outputs, suggested flavors, draft fixtures, and `draft/evals.json` under `skills/<skill>/evals/workspace/create-evals/session-N/`, and only promotes into canonical files on explicit `--promote`.
entries.reuse: When adding authoring or refinement flows to this repo, keep the first pass additive and inspectable so the user can review artifacts before promotion.

entries.title: Conversational Eval Authoring Lessons: Repo-Fit Installation Lesson
entries.lesson: When exposing repo-local skills to Codex, install the repo-shaped set rather than every local skill indiscriminately.
entries.evidence: The local Codex skill install kept `molt-cli`, `eval-author`, `lesson-extractor`, `repo-triage`, `run-summarizer`, `skill-finder`, `skill-refiner`, `python-build`, and `docker-smoke-test`, while leaving game-specific skills out of the default set.
entries.reuse: Treat Codex local skill installation as part of repo ergonomics; pick the subset that matches the repo’s actual workflow and avoid unrelated clusters by default.

entries.title: Conversational Eval Authoring Lessons: Skill-Backed Runtime Lesson
entries.lesson: Repo-specific workflow behavior is easier to evolve when the runtime is backed by a local skill artifact rather than hidden prompt text.
entries.evidence: The new `eval-author` behavior lives in `skills/eval-author/SKILL.md`, while the runtime module focuses on session lifecycle, probe orchestration, and artifact writing.
entries.reuse: When a new capability contains reusable review logic or domain guidance, capture that guidance as a portable skill and keep the runtime layer narrow.

entries.title: Molt CLI Evals Refinement Lessons: Flag Pairing Lesson
entries.lesson: For CLI command skills, pair opt-in flag cases with explicit no-flag cases.
entries.evidence: The existing suite covered `eval-skill ... --baseline snapshot --snapshot-current`, while the new `eval-skill-basic-command` case separately exercised the opposite behavior: do not add snapshot flags when the user asked for a one-off eval.
entries.reuse: When a command has optional flags that materially change behavior, add one eval that requires the flag and another that forbids it unless requested.

entries.title: Molt CLI Evals Refinement Lessons: Grader Fallback Lesson
entries.lesson: Treat `grading.json` entries that say "The grader did not return a result for this check." as grader failures first, not automatic skill regressions.
entries.evidence: In `iteration-2`, the `eval-skill-command` and `eval-skill-basic-command` outputs both contained the expected `./molt skill-builder eval-skill ...` commands and artifact paths in `result.json`, while the corresponding `grading.json` files marked every check failed only because the grader returned no assertions.
entries.scope: command-oriented skill evals that rely on the LLM grader
entries.reuse: When an eval score looks implausibly low, inspect `result.json` alongside `grading.json` before rewriting the skill or the checks, and rerun or simplify the checks if the grader response is empty.

entries.title: Molt CLI Evals Refinement Lessons: Target Workspace Lesson
entries.lesson: Artifact-inspection evals should anchor the answer to the target skill workspace, not just to a generic current case directory.
entries.evidence: The `eval-workspace-case-inspection` case asked about `run-summarizer`, but the with-skill output drifted toward the current eval case folder (`iteration-2/eval-eval-workspace-case-inspection/...`) instead of `skills/run-summarizer/evals/workspace/iteration-N/...`.
entries.scope: skills that tell users where to inspect local eval artifacts
entries.reuse: When authoring skill references or checks for inspection prompts, include the target skill name and expected `skills/<skill>/evals/workspace/...` path pattern explicitly so the response stays attached to the intended workspace.

entries.title: Python Test And Coverage Entry Points Lessons: Coverage Config Lesson
entries.lesson: Put stable test discovery and coverage defaults in `pyproject.toml` so local commands stay short and predictable.
entries.evidence: `tool.pytest.ini_options` now anchors collection to `tests/`, and `tool.coverage.*` defines `moltfarm` as the measured source with missing-line reporting enabled.
entries.reuse: Prefer repo-local configuration over ad hoc shell flags when the goal is repeatable test and coverage behavior for every contributor.

entries.title: Python Test And Coverage Entry Points Lessons: Test Tooling Lesson
entries.lesson: Keep local Python test tooling behind an explicit repo install path instead of assuming `pytest` is globally available.
entries.evidence: The repo now exposes a `test` extra with `pytest` and `pytest-cov`, while the README also preserves ad hoc `uv run --with ...` commands for one-off runs.
entries.reuse: When a repo depends on non-runtime Python tools, expose them through a named extra or similarly explicit install path and document the exact command that uses them.

entries.title: Python Test Coverage Loop Skill Lessons: Command Contract Lesson
entries.lesson: When a repo-specific dev skill needs exact local commands, encode the command contract and the anti-substitution rules directly in the skill instead of leaving command shape implicit.
entries.evidence: `iteration-1` tied on the installed and ad hoc command cases because the with-skill answers drifted to `--cov=.` or omitted `tests`, but `iteration-2` flipped both cases to full wins after the skill and checklist named the exact `python -m pytest tests` and `uv run --with ... --cov=moltfarm` commands and explicitly forbade `uvx`, bare `pytest`, omitted `tests`, and `--cov=.` substitutions.
entries.reuse: When a local repo already has the right command contract, reduce the skill's freedom on that surface and prefer exact command pairs over generalized placeholders.

entries.title: Python Test Coverage Loop Skill Lessons: Eval Interpretation Lesson
entries.lesson: Treat all-grader-empty semantic checks as grader failures first, not as immediate evidence that the skill regressed.
entries.evidence: In `iteration-2`, the `eval-lightweight-cli-test-pattern` with-skill output still described lazy imports, parser or help tests, and zero-findings artifact success, but `grading.json` marked every non-trigger check failed only because each one said `The grader did not return a result for this check.`
entries.reuse: When an eval case suddenly collapses to trigger-only credit, inspect `result.json` alongside `grading.json` before revising the skill or the eval suite.

entries.title: Python Testing Skill Bake-Off Lessons: Bake-Off Reproducibility Lesson
entries.lesson: Imported upstream skills should be kept twice: once as untouched storage, once as uniquely named local eval copies.
entries.evidence: The untouched source copies preserved exact upstream content for inspection, while the renamed local copies allowed `molt skill-builder eval-skill` to run all four skills without name collisions.
entries.reuse: For future comparisons, stage untouched upstream folders under `example_upstream_skills/` and create minimal local eval copies under `skills/` that only change metadata needed for discovery.

entries.title: Python Testing Skill Bake-Off Lessons: Grading Reliability Lesson
entries.lesson: Before trusting close bake-off margins, audit grading fallback rates caused by exact-text alignment between requested checks and grader output.
entries.evidence: This bake-off produced many fallback failures, including `12/18` with-skill checks for `wshobson-python-testing-patterns`, which makes small score differences too noisy to overinterpret.
entries.reuse: Always pair benchmark deltas with a fallback-check audit, and prioritize evaluator hardening before using narrow score gaps to choose a winner.

entries.title: Python Testing Skill Bake-Off Lessons: Near-Dupe Recall Lesson
entries.lesson: The current near-dupe scanner is strong at catching obvious same-name or highly overlapping skills, but it does not yet surface every intuitive family member in a broad skill set.
entries.evidence: In this four-skill pytest bake-off, the scanner surfaced only two candidate pairs and missed every Luxor pairing even though Luxor is clearly part of the same functional family.
entries.scope: upstream skill clustering and sameness detection
entries.reuse: Treat the scanner as an early warning signal, not as a complete clustering pass, especially when long reference-heavy skills may dilute overlap terms.

entries.title: Python Testing Skill Bake-Off Lessons: Pytest Baseline Lesson
entries.lesson: A concise, focused pytest skill can compete well against broader encyclopedic skills when the suite asks for practical answer patterns rather than exhaustive documentation.
entries.evidence: `mindrally-python-testing` slightly outperformed the no-skill baseline and stayed close to Luxor despite being much shorter than Luxor, ECC, or Wshobson.
entries.reuse: The next locally-authored pytest skill should bias toward compact, high-signal guidance and concrete activation cues rather than maximal breadth.

entries.title: Skill Near-Dupe Scanner Lessons: CLI Dependency Lesson
entries.lesson: Keep CLI imports lazy when parser-only or lightweight experimental commands should work without optional runtime dependencies.
entries.evidence: Parser and command tests failed until `cli.py` stopped importing `eval_authoring` and `skill_evaluator` at module import time, because those paths pull in optional packages such as `pydantic`.
entries.reuse: Import heavy or optional command handlers inside the `main()` branch that actually executes them so `--help`, parser tests, and unrelated commands stay usable in narrower environments.

entries.title: Skill Near-Dupe Scanner Lessons: Discovery Shape Lesson
entries.lesson: Preserve one record per discovered `SKILL.md` whenever the task is analysis rather than activation.
entries.evidence: Near-dupe detection needed area metadata and duplicate-name preservation, which the existing name-keyed `discover_skills()` map could not provide because it collapses collisions.
entries.reuse: For reporting, diffing, and overlap detection work, start from a list-based discovery helper and only collapse to a dict at the last moment if the caller truly needs unique names.

entries.title: Skill Near-Dupe Scanner Lessons: Threshold Discipline Lesson
entries.lesson: Separate “candidate found” from “analysis succeeded” when a conservative heuristic is intentionally allowed to return zero matches.
entries.evidence: The real smoke run over this repo's current `skills/` tree completed successfully and wrote a report, but produced zero pairs because the planned `>= 0.50` threshold stayed conservative.
entries.reuse: For advisory analyzers, always emit an inspectable artifact and a successful exit path even when the current corpus yields no findings, then let threshold tuning happen explicitly in a later refinement.

entries.title: Targeted Helper Tests For Coverage: Coverage Lesson
entries.lesson: When a local-first module has broad orchestration but many pure helpers, raise coverage primarily with direct helper tests plus a few thin dispatch tests instead of forcing more large end-to-end setups.
entries.evidence: Covering CLI dispatch arms directly and testing runner/eval-authoring normalization helpers in isolation moved total coverage from `82%` to `94%` without runtime refactors or broader fixture sprawl.
entries.reuse: Prefer small file-backed fixtures and direct helper assertions for validation, normalization, import, and path-handling branches; keep end-to-end tests for only the orchestration seams that actually need them.

entries.title: Pytest Suite Ceiling After Grader Hardening: Discriminative Suite Lesson
entries.lesson: To distinguish a specialist pytest skill from a strong foundation model, eval cases must demand more than generic correctness.
entries.evidence: The six-case suite covered practical pytest patterns well, but after grading was hardened the no-skill baseline still satisfied every case.
entries.reuse: Add cases or checks that reward compactness, sharper tool choice, anti-pattern avoidance, or repo-specific precision if the goal is to separate strong skills from a strong general baseline.

entries.title: Pytest Suite Ceiling After Grader Hardening: Fair Recomparison Lesson
entries.lesson: Any leaderboard produced before a grader-behavior change should be treated as historical, not directly comparable to post-fix runs.
entries.evidence: The original upstream pytest bake-off highlighted Luxor and Mindrally, but the post-fix `python-pytest-essentials` run used a materially different grading path and therefore cannot be ranked fairly against those earlier numbers without rerunning them.
entries.reuse: After changing grader alignment, rerun the top comparison set or explicitly label the new run as a follow-up on a changed eval surface.

entries.title: Pytest Suite Ceiling After Grader Hardening: Grader Hardening Lesson
entries.lesson: Recovering paraphrased grader outputs is necessary, but once that recovery is in place, a generic eval suite may stop separating skill quality from baseline capability.
entries.evidence: After hardening grading alignment and rerunning the six-case pytest suite for `python-pytest-essentials`, both the with-skill and without-skill configurations scored a perfect `1.0` weighted pass rate across all six cases.
entries.reuse: When a suite suddenly becomes all ties after a grader fix, treat that as evidence that the suite is saturated, not that every skill is equally good.

entries.title: Local Gemma Pure-Local Pilot Lessons: Basic-Skill Pilot Lesson
entries.lesson: Small text-only skills are sufficient to validate a local model’s practical skill path before attempting full eval loops or local grading.
entries.evidence: Both direct and proxy-backed subject runs showed `function_call:activate_skill:*` and `function_call:read_skill_resource:*` for `repo-triage`, `run-summarizer`, and `docker-smoke-test`, while full local grading remained out of scope.
entries.reuse: Start local-model validation with narrow text-only skills and judge success from trace artifacts first; do not block the pilot on local evaluator grading.

entries.title: Local Gemma Pure-Local Pilot Lessons: Direct-First Lesson
entries.lesson: For a local-model pilot, make direct chat-completions the baseline path and treat proxy-backed Responses as a second surface.
entries.evidence: Gemma worked immediately on the direct `llama.cpp` surface for `get_weather`, the full `exec_command` capability probe, and the three basic skill runs, while the proxy path required additional compatibility fixes before the same workflows passed.
entries.reuse: When adding a new local backend, prove the direct OpenAI-compatible chat path first, then layer a Responses proxy on top only after tool calling is already known-good.

entries.title: Local Gemma Pure-Local Pilot Lessons: Proxy Health And Auth Lesson
entries.lesson: A local Responses proxy must handle authenticated upstreams explicitly and expose the health contract the caller expects.
entries.evidence: The first proxy attempts failed until the proxy accepted `MOLT_UPSTREAM_API_KEY`, forwarded upstream auth, and exposed `/health` in addition to `/healthz`, while the runtime also had to treat `GET /v1/responses` returning `405` as a healthy Responses preflight.
entries.scope: proxy compatibility with local OpenAI-compatible servers
entries.reuse: When fronting an authenticated local model server, wire explicit upstream auth and support both health aliases before debugging higher-level tool behavior.

entries.title: Local Gemma Pure-Local Pilot Lessons: Untyped Responses Input Lesson
entries.lesson: The OpenAI Responses client may send message items as plain `{"role": ..., "content": ...}` objects inside list input, so a proxy cannot assume every item has an explicit `"type"`.
entries.evidence: The first real `openai_responses` workflow failed with a proxy `500` because the translator only accepted typed items; normalizing role/content pairs into `type="message"` fixed the failure and allowed the `manual-triage`, `manual-run-summary`, and `manual-docker-smoke-test` workflows to complete.
entries.reuse: Accept the SDK-style untyped message shape and convert any remaining input-shape errors into structured `400` responses instead of leaking internal server errors.

entries.title: Skill Authoring And Wiki Validation Lessons: Grader-Fallback Lesson
entries.lesson: Invalid local grader payloads should downgrade the affected checks to failed grading instead of aborting the whole eval iteration.
entries.evidence: After normalizing alternate summary keys and adding an error fallback in `packages/moltfarm/skill_evaluator.py`, the local `llm-wiki-validator` suite completed and wrote `benchmark.json`, `feedback.json`, and per-case `comparison.json` even though local grader outputs still varied in shape.
entries.reuse: When local models are part of the grading path, preserve inspectable iteration artifacts by turning malformed grader output into explicit failed assertions rather than a fatal exception.

entries.title: Skill Authoring And Wiki Validation Lessons: Local Grader Schema Lesson
entries.lesson: A local `openai_compatible` model can drive subject runs, but full `eval-skill` still needs a grader that emits the repo's exact `GradingPayload` summary schema or a normalization layer in front of it.
entries.evidence: Local Gemma completed subject outputs for both new skills, but both `eval-skill` runs failed when the local grader returned JSON without `summary.passed`, `summary.failed`, `summary.total`, and `summary.pass_rate`.
entries.reuse: Keep a cloud grader or add schema normalization before claiming a fully local `eval-skill` loop.

entries.title: Skill Authoring And Wiki Validation Lessons: Manual-First Eval Lesson
entries.lesson: New skills still need hand-authored canonical evals as a first-class path because `create-evals` can be blocked by external quota or probe-runtime failures.
entries.evidence: `./molt skill-builder create-evals molt-skill-builder-authoring` wrote a reviewable `session-1` workspace but failed on repeated `429 insufficient_quota`, and its `probe-primary-task` observation also recorded an `OSError: [Errno 36] File name too long` failure in `probe-observations.json`.
entries.reuse: Author `SKILL.md`, fixtures, and canonical `evals/evals.json` directly first; treat `create-evals` as an additive drafting aid rather than the only route to a shippable skill.

entries.title: Skill Authoring And Wiki Validation Lessons: Short-Step Authoring Lesson
entries.lesson: Authoring-loop skills need an explicit "short ordered actions and commands" instruction to keep local-model responses operational instead of essay-like.
entries.evidence: The first local subject output for `molt-skill-builder-authoring` got the sequence right but expanded into headings and narrative in `iteration-1/.../outputs/summary.txt`, which led to a follow-up tightening of the skill instructions.
entries.reuse: When evals expect concise workflow guidance, tell the skill to answer as a short ordered sequence of actions and commands.

entries.title: Skill Authoring And Wiki Validation Lessons: Source-Path Wiki Lesson
entries.lesson: Wiki-building skills should require page-level update plans to name the exact supporting note, reference, or errata path for each non-obvious fact.
entries.evidence: The partial local grading trace for `llm-wiki` passed the routing and structure checks but missed the evidence-linking check because the plan named the destination pages without explicitly tying each step back to the raw-fragment source.
entries.reuse: When a skill proposes wiki updates, require the destination page and supporting source path in the same step so evidence discipline survives summarization.

entries.title: Workflow-First System Map From Lessons: Draft-Then-Promote Knowledge Layer Lesson
entries.lesson: Curated knowledge layers should write a reviewable draft workspace before they touch canonical wiki pages or promoted indexes.
entries.evidence: The new `manual-system-map-draft` flow writes `plan.md`, candidate pages, and a draft `lesson-index.json` under `wiki/drafts/session-N/`, while promotion is a separate `promote-system-map` step.
entries.reuse: When adding a new synthesis surface in Molt, keep the first pass additive and inspectable so raw evidence stays untouched until the curated layer is reviewed.

entries.title: Workflow-First System Map From Lessons: Structured Retrieval Lesson
entries.lesson: A promoted knowledge index should preserve supporting file paths so authoring and refinement flows can retrieve the right raw lesson even when the lesson prose is generic.
entries.evidence: The promoted `wiki/_build/lesson-index.json` now stores `supporting_paths`, and both authoring and refinement lookups consult that index before falling back to raw lesson-file substring matching.
entries.scope: lesson retrieval for authoring and refinement
entries.reuse: When a curated knowledge layer drives retrieval, store the raw lesson path plus enough structured references to route context without re-reading the whole corpus every time.
